g1u08.w.to-borrow.note: SENSE imprecision in d: "to have it for a short time and then give it back to a friend." You give it back to the OWNER you took it from (here a friend), but the phrasing reads as giving it to some friend, blurring the borrow-from relationship and edging toward 'lend'. Defensible given the carrier ("Can I borrow your red sweater?") but worth a human look.

g1u08.w.hoodie.note: SENSE imprecision in d: "It is like a sweater and you can pull it up over your hair." It is the HOOD you pull up over your hair, not the whole hoodie ("it" = the garment). Recoverable for a child and the sweater item leans on this contrast, but literally imprecise.
